meta.title: X-Cache · Cross-Chunk Block Caching
meta.description: A training-free, cross-chunk caching method for few-step autoregressive driving world models. 71% block skip, 2.6× wall-clock speedup, no visible quality loss.
meta.ogAlt: X-Cache: cross-chunk block caching for autoregressive video diffusion.

hero.title: X-Cache
hero.subtitle: Cross-Chunk Block Caching for Few-Step Autoregressive World Models
hero.lede: Generate the next chunk of a 7-camera driving world without paying full DiT compute. X-Cache reuses block residuals across consecutive chunks instead of across denoising steps — surviving few-step distillation that erases every other caching trick.

intro.title: Real-time world simulation breaks every existing cache.
intro.p1: Autoregressive video diffusion is the natural backbone for an interactive driving world: it streams chunk by chunk, conditions on a live policy's actions, and never waits for a full clip. To meet real-time budgets, models like X-World are distilled to four denoising steps.
intro.p2: That distillation collapses the redundancy that every prior cache relies on. TeaCache, DeepCache, ΔDiT, BWCache — all reuse block outputs across denoising steps. With S = 4, each step contributes substantial, non-redundant structural updates. There is nothing left to skip on that axis.
intro.p3: Worse, the per-chunk action stream is intentionally non-smooth at chunk boundaries (braking, steering, lane-change), and closed-loop generation forbids look-ahead, ruling out trajectory extrapolation and block-cascading parallelism. Every familiar acceleration tool fails here.

principle.title: Cache along a different axis.
principle.lede: Driving scenes evolve smoothly relative to the generation rate. Two consecutive chunks describe almost the same physical world. The block input at position (t, b) at chunk n is therefore highly similar to the same (t, b) at chunk n−1 — even when there are only four denoising steps inside.
principle.axisStepDesc: Reuse block outputs between adjacent denoising steps. Erased by few-step distillation.
principle.axisChunkDesc: Reuse block residuals between adjacent generation chunks. Comes from physical scene continuity, survives any S.
principle.diagramCaption: Block residuals from chunk n are written into a per-(t, b) cache. At chunk n+1, the gate decides per block whether to recompute or reuse.
principle.equationCaption: Approximate the next chunk's block output by adding the cached residual to the current block input.

principlePoints.label: Block-level residual cache
principlePoints.body: Per (denoising step t, block b) slot. The cache key is the block position, not the timestep, so reuse always happens at matching positions.

principlePoints.label: Structure & action-aware fingerprint
principlePoints.body: Subsamples block inputs on the 3D (F, H, W) latent grid (32 tokens), plus a global-mean channel and a flattened action vector. Closed-form, ~free to compute.

principlePoints.label: Dual-metric gate
principlePoints.body: Cosine similarity (global direction) AND maximum token deviation (local outlier). A block is skipped only if both pass — anomalies in any view group veto reuse.

principlePoints.label: Per-(t, b) adaptive threshold
principlePoints.body: An EMA of each cell's own cosine history sets the threshold from below; a hard floor τ = 0.97 guarantees a quality margin.

architecture.title: What happens inside one chunk.
architecture.stepsHeading: From noise to a 7-camera chunk, in four passes.

steps.title: Sample noise & init context
steps.body: Each chunk starts from Gaussian noise; the rolling KV cache supplies the long-horizon history. The current action vector enters every block via adaLN-Zero.

steps.title: Compute fingerprints
steps.body: Per block input, sample 32 tokens on the 3D latent grid (proportional to Fg : Hg : Wg). Append the global mean and the action vector. Cost: a single index-and-flatten.

steps.title: Gate decision
steps.body: Compare current fingerprint to the cached fingerprint at (t, b). Skip iff cosine ≥ τ_cos(t, b) AND max-deviation < τ_dev. Otherwise compute the block and refresh the cache.

steps.title: KV update — protected
steps.body: After the last denoising step, X-World writes a clean key/value into the persistent cache. On that chunk we force-compute every block, refusing to let approximation contaminate the autoregressive context.

architecture.schemaCaption: End-to-end view: fingerprint → dual-metric gate → residual reuse vs. full compute → KV-update guarded recompute.

safety.title: Four guardrails that keep approximation contained.

items.title: KV-update protection
items.body: The pass that writes clean K/V into the rolling cache is force-computed in full. Skipping here is the only knob that materially breaks quality (PSNR drops from 53.4 → 21.5 dB).

items.title: Anchor block (F_n = 1)
items.body: Block 0 always recomputes, so the fresh action and conditioning cascade into every downstream block's fingerprint and naturally reset stale chains.

items.title: Step-0 protection (optional)
items.body: Off by default — the cosine gate already vetoes step 0 reuse where it would matter. Available as an extra margin under heavy distribution shift.

items.title: Adaptive threshold floor
items.body: τ_floor = 0.97 caps how loose the per-cell EMA threshold can drift. Inactive on the current workload, but armed for tails.

demo.title: Drag the curtain. There is no quality drop.
demo.lede: Three scenarios, one toggle. Sweep the seam to compare baseline full-compute (left) and X-Cache (right) side by side, switch into the 20×-amplified diff to inspect every pixel of approximation error, or play them in shadow mode to overlay both at once.

scenarios.title: Dense traffic, pedestrians, storefronts.
scenarios.body: Seven urban clips with the highest static texture density in the test split. The cosine gate runs near-saturated; skip rate stays at 71.4% and the residual sits on lane edges and far-field foliage.

scenarios.title: Elevated express ring & ordinary motorway.
scenarios.body: Three clips. Long depth of field, rapid forward motion, sparse foreground. The gate skips 71.6% of blocks — and decoded 7-cam PSNR climbs to 54.7 dB because most pixels are sky/asphalt that absorb latent perturbations cleanly.

scenarios.title: Maximum cross-chunk motion in the split.
scenarios.body: Three clips where the ego vehicle executes a sharp heading change. Adjacent chunks are the most different in the dataset — yet the cross-chunk fingerprint still survives, with skip rate 71.3% and no chunk-boundary drift visible in the per-frame PSNR trace.

results.title: Same seed, same conditioning, 2.6× faster — and the pixels prove it.
results.tableCaption: Per-scenario summary against the no-cache baseline. Per-camera rows are F-C (front centre), F-W (front wide), S-FL/FR/RL/RR (sides) and Rear; the highlighted 7-cam row is the stitched 7-view image used in the qualitative figures.
results.ablationCaption: One protection mechanism removed at a time on a held-out highway clip. KV-update protection is the only hard requirement; every other knob is a margin-of-safety knob.
results.skipBody: All three scenarios follow nearly the same curve: blocks 1–19 sit on the 0.75 plateau implied by W = 1 warmup over a 4-chunk reuse window; blocks 20–26 fall to ≈ 0.69. The 0.3-pp spread between scenarios comes from movement inside this fixed set of blocks, not from scene content.

footer.note: Site code, generation scripts, and the full report live in this repo. Videos are produced from the held-out test rollouts under wm_v2.0.
